data.title: Sensex, Nifty continue to trade lower as IT stocks tumble; mid, smallcap indices rise nearly 2%
data.description:
Bharti Airtel, IndusInd Bank, Shriram Finance, SBI, and HCL Tech were the major laggards on the index.

Benchmark indices Nifty and Sensex extended losses on May 16, dragged down by a sharp selloff in IT, pharma, and banking stocks that weighed on overall sentiment. However, the broader market remained resilient, with mid- and small-cap stocks continuing to outperform their large-cap peers.

At noon, the Sensex was down 309.81 points or 0.38 percent at 82,220.93, and the Nifty was down 85.10 points or 0.34 percent at 24,977.00. About 2218 shares advanced, 1180 shares declined, and 122 shares remained unchanged.

Follow our LIVE blog for all the latest market updates

Despite worries that foreign investors might pull back from Indian equities in favour of cheaper Chinese stocks amid progress on a US-China trade deal, they took the market by surprise with strong buying worth Rs 5,393 crore in yesterday's session. "The unexpected inflow triggered a wave of short covering in largecaps, powering a sharp rally. With momentum now tilting towards largecaps, the market could continue its upward move if foreign inflows persist, though rising valuations may start to weigh on fundamentals," V K Vijayakumar, Chief Investment Strategist at Geojit Financial Services, said.

Sectoral trends on the NSE were mixed on May 15, with broader markets continuing to outperform. The Nifty Smallcap 100 and Midcap 100 indices rose 1.39 percent and 0.76 percent, respectively.

Also read: Mutual funds cut exposure to Adani Group, shares worth over Rs 1,160 cr sold in April

The Nifty Realty index led sectoral gainers with a 1.44 percent jump. Gains were also seen in Nifty Energy (0.72 percent), Media (0.57 percent), Auto (0.38 percent), Consumer Durables (0.35 percent), FMCG (0.31 percent), and PSU Bank (0.19 percent). On the flip side, Nifty IT slipped 1.02 percent, followed by losses in Pharma, Metal, Infra, Bank, and Private Bank indices. Meanwhile, market volatility eased, with India VIX falling 1.72 percent to 16.60.

In the afternoon, defence sector stocks rose sharply for the sixth consecutive session on Friday, with Cochin Shipyard, Mazagon Dock Shipbuilders and Bharat Electronics Ltd (BEL) among the top gainers, as investors cheered the government’s renewed thrust on indigenous defence manufacturing. The Nifty India Defence index hit a fresh 52-week high during the session today, rising to 20 percent since May 9.

IndusInd Bank shares were down 3 percent as brokerages turned more cautious after the lender disclosed two new accounting lapses. Analysts have not only downgraded the stock but also flagged sharp cuts to earnings estimates for FY25 through FY27. In a regulatory filing on Thursday evening, the private lender said that its Internal Audit Committee (IAD) was conducting a review of the bank's MFI business to examine certain concerns brought to the bank's attention.

Read more: Bharti Airtel large trade deal: 1.3% equity change hands at Rs 1,820 per share

Swiggy, Zomato shares gained 3 percent after the food delivery majors reduced membership benefits ahead of monsoon season. Swiggy One and Zomato Gold users now face a "rain surcharge" on food orders. The firms were earlier levying no surcharge to customers with membership benefits. According to the latest update, Zomato Gold members/Swiggy One users will be treated on par with non-members. The move comes at a time when Zomato and Swiggy are both under immense investor pressure to improve their profit profile.

"Nifty has triggered a breakout and has opened the gates for fresh upside targets of 25,400 and 25,800 levels, with major support now positioned near the 24,500 level, which needs to be sustained to keep the overall bias intact. Sensex continued with the positive move, extending the gains and breaching above the 82,000 zone with bias getting strong, and in the coming sessions is aiming for the 84000 landmark," Prabhudas Lilladher said in a note. "The undertone has become strong, with the broader markets actively participating in the rally, supporting the benchmark indices with near-term support maintained near the important 20DMA zone at the 80,200 level, which needs to be sustained as of now," the brokerage added.

Bharat Electronics, NTPC, Eicher Motors, Bajaj Auto and Adani Enterprises were the top gainers on the Nifty. Bharti Airtel, IndusInd Bank, Shriram Finance, SBI, and HCL Tech were the major laggards on the index.

Disclaimer: The views and investment tips expressed by experts on Moneycontrol are their own and not those of the website or its management. Moneycontrol advises users to consult certified experts before making any investment decisions.
data.author: MoneyControl

Bharat Electronics shares experienced a significant surge in today's session, rising by 4.14% to reach Rs 364.90. Bharat Electronics stock hit an all-time high of Rs 366.6 in today's trading. This increase highlights strong market activity for the stock, which is part of the NIFTY 50 STOCKS index.

The positive movement in Bharat Electronics' stock price is complemented by a very bullish sentiment analysis from Moneycontrol as of May 16, 2025, which may reflect growing investor confidence in the company.
